title: salix × subcaesia
intro:
Salix × subcaesia, also known as hybrid willow or hybrid sallow, is a hybrid plant between the white willow (Salix alba) and the goat willow (Salix caprea), characterized by its distinctive leaves and slender branches.

The medicinal properties of Salix × subcaesia lie in its bark, which contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, making it a natural anti-inflammatory agent. This plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including pain, fever, and rheumatism, due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

The health benefits of Salix × subcaesia include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and lower fever, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Additionally, its antiseptic and antibacterial properties make it effective in treating wounds and preventing infection.

Salix × subcaesia can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
